sense.definition: a room that students are taught in, particularly in a college, school, or university

examples.x: I keep my school supplies organized in my backpack for the classroom.
She enjoys decorating the classroom with colorful artwork.
The classroom is filled with desks, chairs, and a chalkboard.
They work on group projects together in the classroom.
We have a class discussion in the classroom to share our ideas.

sense.definition: a room for all teachers of a school to go to take a break, relax, and socialize with their colleagues

examples.x: The staffroom is where I go when I need to catch up on paperwork during free periods.
I accidentally left my lunch in the staffroom fridge and had to go back to get it.
The staffroom is often crowded in the mornings as everyone prepares for the day ahead.
She always takes her coffee break in the staffroom with the other school employees.
We hold our monthly meetings in the staffroom to discuss school-wide issues.

sense.definition: a special room in a school where teachers can relax and socialize when they are not teaching

examples.x: The faculty lounge serves as a hub for intellectual exchange and camaraderie among educators.
She frequents the faculty lounge to engage in stimulating conversations with her colleagues.
The inviting ambiance of the faculty lounge fosters a sense of community and collaboration among the teaching staff.
He finds solace in the serene environment of the faculty lounge, allowing him to recharge between classes.
The faculty lounge provides a sanctuary for educators to decompress and recharge amidst their demanding schedules.

sense.definition: a room in a school, etc. that contains lockers in which people can change their clothes

examples.x: The football players gathered in the locker room to discuss strategy before the big game.
She left her gym bag in the locker room while she worked out on the treadmill.
The coach gave a motivational speech to the team in the locker room during halftime.
Athletes are expected to keep the locker room clean and tidy at all times.
The basketball team celebrated their victory in the locker room after the championship game.

sense.definition: a shared space in a building, like a school or residence, where people gather for socializing or relaxing

examples.x: The common room provided a comfortable space for residents to watch movies or play games together.
Teachers occasionally used the common room for informal meetings or discussions with students.
The common room was furnished with sofas, tables, and vending machines for convenience.
During study breaks, students often grabbed snacks from the common room before returning to their work.
Students gathered in the common room between classes to chat and unwind.

sense.definition: a large room or building within a school or university used for assemblies, lectures, performances, or dining

examples.x: The school assembly was held in the main hall every Monday morning.
Students gathered in the hall for the annual talent show, showcasing their musical and artistic talents.
The university hall was equipped with audiovisual technology for lectures and presentations.
The dining hall served nutritious meals to students and staff throughout the school day.
Graduation ceremonies were held in the grand hall, filled with proud parents and faculty.

sense.definition: a designated space within a school where students can work on homework or study independently under supervision

examples.x: The study hall provided a quiet environment for students to concentrate on their assignments and readings.
Students filed into the study hall after lunch, taking their seats at individual desks arranged in rows.
The study hall was equipped with ample lighting and comfortable seating to facilitate focused study sessions.
During study hall, students were expected to refrain from talking and remain focused on their academic tasks.
The teacher on duty circulated around the study hall, ensuring that students were engaged in productive study activities.

sense.definition: a large classroom in a school or university where teachers give lessons or presentations to many students at once

examples.x: Students attend lectures in the university's lecture hall, which can seat hundreds of people.
She listens to her professor's lectures on history in the lecture hall every week.
The lecture hall is equipped with audiovisual aids to enhance teaching.
He presented his research findings to an audience of students and faculty in the lecture hall.
The school renovated the lecture hall to improve comfort and technology for students and teachers.

sense.definition: a place in which collections of books and sometimes newspapers, movies, music, etc. are kept for people to read or borrow

examples.x: I spent the afternoon studying at the local library.
You can borrow novels, DVDs, and magazines from the library.
The university library has an extensive collection of academic journals.
I returned the books I borrowed to the library yesterday.
The library hosts regular storytelling sessions for children.

sense.definition: a place where people do scientific experiments, manufacture drugs, etc.

examples.x: The chemistry students conducted experiments in the laboratory to study chemical reactions.
Pharmaceutical companies use laboratories to develop and test new medications.
Forensic laboratories analyze evidence collected from crime scenes to aid in investigations.
Environmental scientists monitor water quality in their laboratory to assess pollution levels.
Food scientists work in laboratories to develop new food products and improve food safety standards.

sense.definition: a specialized facility equipped with audio and visual aids, used for language learning and practice

examples.x: The university's language laboratory offers interactive exercises for students to improve their language skills.
The language laboratory provides resources such as language software and multimedia materials for self-study.
He spends time in the language laboratory to work on pronunciation and listening comprehension.
The school upgraded its language laboratory with digital language learning tools and online resources.
Students utilize the language laboratory to enhance their proficiency through immersive language exercises and conversation practice.

sense.definition: the part of a theater, concert hall, or other venue where the audience sits to watch a performance

examples.x: The auditorium was filled to capacity with eager spectators awaiting the start of the theatrical production.
The speaker's voice reverberated throughout the vast auditorium as they addressed the audience on important matters.
The school assembly was held in the auditorium, where students gathered to listen to announcements and performances.
The orchestra performed a mesmerizing concert in the acoustically excellent auditorium, captivating the audience with their music.
The company's annual conference took place in the modern auditorium, equipped with state-of-the-art audiovisual technology for presentations.

sense.definition: a place with special equipment that people go to exercise or play sports

examples.x: He goes to the gym five times a week.
The gym was crowded with people after work.
She joined a new gym near her house.
They offer yoga classes at the gym.
I saw her lifting weights at the gym yesterday.

sense.definition: a small room or building belonging to a hospital, prison, school, etc. where Christians can pray and perform religious services in
a space equipped with computers and technology for educational or training purposes

examples.x: Students use the computer lab to complete assignments and research.
She attends programming classes in the computer lab.
The university's computer lab offers resources for students to develop digital skills.
Employees undergo software training in the company's computer lab.
The computer lab is available for community members to access technology and learn new skills.

sense.definition: a web-based platform for delivering digital content in educational institutions
a college or university building in which students can reside

examples.x: She lived in a residence hall during her freshman year of college.
The residence hall offers various amenities such as lounges, study areas, and laundry facilities.
The university assigned roommates to students living in the residence hall based on compatibility.
Residents of the residence hall must adhere to community guidelines and regulations.
The residence hall staff organizes social events and activities to foster a sense of community among residents.

sense.definition: a college or university building in which students reside

examples.x: He made many friends while living in the dormitory during his freshman year.
The dormitory is located near the library and the student center for easy access.

sense.definition: an outdoor area within a school's premises where students can gather, play, and socialize during breaks or recess

examples.x: Children ran around the schoolyard, playing tag and kicking a soccer ball.
Teachers supervised students in the schoolyard to ensure their safety during recess.
The schoolyard was equipped with playground equipment such as swings, slides, and monkey bars.
Students enjoyed eating lunch outdoors on benches scattered throughout the schoolyard.
During warm weather, classes sometimes held outdoor lessons in the schoolyard to provide a change of scenery.

sense.definition: a playing area built outdoors for children, particularly inside parks or schools

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon playing at the playground.
The school built a new playground with a climbing wall.
Parents watched their kids run around the playground.
The playground includes swings, slides, and a sandbox.
Safety mats were installed under the equipment in the playground.

sense.definition: a facility within an institution, such as a school or hospital, where medical treatment and care are provided to patients who are ill or injured

examples.x: Sarah volunteered at the local infirmary every weekend, assisting the nurses with basic tasks.

sense.definition: a department within a university that provides mental health services, including therapy and support, to students dealing with emotional, psychological, or academic challenges

examples.x: The university counseling center offers individual and group therapy sessions to students struggling with anxiety and depression.
She visited the university counseling center for guidance on managing academic stress and improving study habits.
He sought assistance from the university counseling center to navigate personal relationships and enhance communication skills.
The university counseling center offers crisis intervention services for students in urgent need of mental health support.
Students can schedule confidential appointments at the university counseling center to discuss a wide range of concerns, from homesickness to identity exploration.

sense.definition: a part of an organization such as a university, government, etc. that deals with a particular task

examples.x: The health department issued a warning about the flu outbreak.

sense.definition: a geographical area served by a single school system, typically overseen by a local government or educational authority

examples.x: The school district implemented new policies to improve academic performance and student outcomes.
Parents attended a meeting to discuss proposed changes to school district boundaries.
The school district provided transportation services to students residing in remote areas.
The superintendent of the school district announced plans for constructing a new elementary school to accommodate population growth.
The school district's board of education voted on budget allocations for various educational programs and initiatives.

sense.definition: a four-sided courtyard or open space, often enclosed by buildings or walls, typically found in educational institutions, residential complexes, or historical landmarks
a place where children learn things from teachers

examples.x: He forgot his homework and had to rush back to school to get it.
My children go to school to learn new things and make friends.
She takes the bus to school every morning.
We have a science project due next week at school.
We study different subjects like math, science, and English at school.

sense.definition: an area of land in which a university, college, or school, along with all their buildings, are situated

examples.x: The campus of the university is spread out over 100 acres of land.
Students often gather in the quad on campus to study or socialize.
There are several dining options available on campus for students and faculty.
The campus is known for its beautiful landscaping and green spaces.
Security patrols the campus to ensure the safety of students and staff.

sense.definition: a college or school that provides people with special training
an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study and practice of music

examples.x: Students enrolled in the Department of Music have the opportunity to participate in various ensembles and recitals.
The Department of Music hosts concerts and recitals featuring both faculty and student performers throughout the academic year.
She majored in music composition within the Department of Music, studying under renowned composers and theorists.
The Department of Music offers a wide range of courses, including music theory, musicology, and ethnomusicology.
The Department of Music faculty consists of accomplished musicians and scholars who are actively engaged in research and performance.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of human society

examples.x: The Department of Sociology offers a diverse range of courses exploring topics such as inequality, globalization, and social change.
As part of the Department of Sociology, students conduct research on pressing social issues, contributing to our understanding of society.
The Department of Sociology hosts lectures and seminars featuring prominent scholars and researchers in the field.
She decided to major in sociology within the Department of Sociology to better understand the complexities of human interaction and social structures.
The Department of Sociology faculty comprises experts in various subfields, including criminology, demography, and urban sociology.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of human societies, cultures, and behaviors

examples.x: The Department of Anthropology conducts fieldwork and research projects around the world to better understand the diversity of human cultures and societies.
As part of the Department of Anthropology, students explore topics such as kinship, religion, and ecology, gaining insights into the complexities of human existence.
The Department of Anthropology sponsors guest lectures and symposiums featuring leading scholars and experts in the field.
She pursued a degree in anthropology within the Department of Anthropology, combining coursework with hands-on field experience.

The Department of Anthropology faculty includes specialists in various subfields, such as forensic anthropology, primatology, and medical anthropology, who contribute to both teaching and research endeavors.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of living organisms and their interactions

examples.x: The Department of Biology conducts cutting-edge research in areas such as molecular biology, biotechnology, and conservation biology.
Students in the Department of Biology have access to state-of-the-art laboratories and field research opportunities to explore the diversity of life.
The Department of Biology sponsors seminars and workshops featuring leading scientists and researchers from around the world.
She majored in biology within the Department of Biology, focusing on her passion for marine biology and environmental science.
The Department of Biology faculty comprises experts in various disciplines, including botany, zoology, microbiology, and neuroscience, who are dedicated to both teaching and research.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college focused on the study of chemical substances, their properties, and transformations

examples.x: The Department of Chemistry offers courses in organic, inorganic, and physical chemistry.
Students in the Department of Chemistry conduct experiments in well-equipped laboratories.
The Department of Chemistry hosts seminars on cutting-edge research topics.
She pursued a degree in chemistry within the Department of Chemistry.
The Department of Chemistry faculty consists of experts in various subfields.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of computers, computational systems, and software

examples.x: The Department of Computer Science conducts research in areas such as machine learning, cybersecurity, and computer vision.
Students in the Department of Computer Science learn programming skills and problem-solving techniques to develop software applications and systems.
The Department of Computer Science organizes workshops and hackathons to foster creativity and innovation among students.
He majored in computer science within the Department of Computer Science, focusing on his interest in data science and analytics.
The Department of Computer Science faculty includes experts in various fields, such as software engineering, human-computer interaction, and computer graphics.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of English language, literature, and composition

examples.x: The Department of English hosts lectures and readings by acclaimed authors and scholars.
Students in the Department of English analyze works of fiction, poetry, and drama from different historical periods and cultural contexts.
The Department of English offers workshops and seminars on writing skills and literary criticism.
She pursued a degree in English within the Department of English, exploring her passion for Shakespearean literature and modern poetry.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college focused on the study of past events, societies, and cultures

examples.x: The Department of History conducts research in areas such as political history, social history, and cultural history, contributing to our understanding of the past.
Students in the Department of History analyze primary sources, develop research skills, and critically evaluate historical interpretations.
The Department of History organizes conferences and symposiums where scholars and students present their research findings and engage in academic discussions.
He majored in history within the Department of History, fascinated by the complexities of historical narratives and their relevance to contemporary issues.
The Department of History faculty includes distinguished historians and researchers who are dedicated to teaching and mentoring students in the study of the past.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of numbers, shapes, and patterns

examples.x: The Department of Mathematics conducts research in areas such as pure mathematics, applied mathematics, and mathematical modeling.
Students in the Department of Mathematics develop problem-solving skills and logical reasoning abilities through coursework and problem sets.
The Department of Mathematics organizes math competitions and seminars to promote interest and excellence in mathematics among students.
She pursued a degree in mathematics within the Department of Mathematics, inspired by her passion for mathematical theory and its applications.
The Department of Mathematics faculty includes experts in various mathematical disciplines, such as number theory, differential equations, and mathematical physics.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college focused on the scientific study of language and its structure

examples.x: The Department of Linguistics conducts research in areas such as language acquisition, language variation, and computational linguistics.
Students in the Department of Linguistics analyze the sounds, grammar, and meaning of languages, gaining insights into human communication.
The Department of Linguistics sponsors workshops and conferences where scholars and students present their research findings and exchange ideas.
He majored in linguistics within the Department of Linguistics, intrigued by the diversity of languages and their underlying systems.
The Department of Linguistics faculty includes renowned linguists and researchers who are passionate about exploring the structure and function of language across different cultures and contexts.

sense.definition: a division of a school or university that is responsible for teaching and research related to physics

examples.x: She is a professor in the Department of Physics.
The Department of Physics organizes an annual science fair.
He submitted his research paper to the Department of Physics.
The Department of Physics offers courses on quantum mechanics.
The university’s Department of Physics is well-known for its research.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college focused on the study of how societies allocate resources and make decisions

examples.x: The Department of Economics conducts research in areas such as economic policy, development economics, and behavioral economics.
Students in the Department of Economics analyze economic data, study economic models, and learn about economic phenomena such as inflation, unemployment, and market competition.
The Department of Economics hosts seminars and conferences where scholars and students discuss current economic issues and research findings.
She pursued a degree in economics within the Department of Economics, fascinated by the dynamics of markets and the role of government in the economy.
The Department of Economics faculty includes experts in various fields, such as international trade, labor economics, and financial economics.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, ethics, and reality

examples.x: The Department of Philosophy conducts research in areas such as philosophy of mind, moral philosophy, and political philosophy.
Students in the Department of Philosophy engage in critical thinking, argumentation, and the analysis of philosophical texts and ideas.
The Department of Philosophy sponsors colloquia and workshops where faculty and students present their research and engage in philosophical discussions.
She pursued a degree in philosophy within the Department of Philosophy, drawn to the exploration of life's big questions and the pursuit of wisdom.
The Department of Philosophy faculty includes distinguished philosophers and scholars who are committed to both teaching and advancing knowledge in philosophy.

sense.definition: an academic division within a university or college dedicated to the scientific study of human behavior and mental processes

examples.x: The Department of Psychology conducts research in areas such as cognition, emotion, and personality, contributing to our understanding of human behavior.
Students in the Department of Psychology learn about research methods, statistical analysis, and psychological theories through coursework and hands-on experience.
The Department of Psychology provides clinical services and counseling to individuals and communities, addressing mental health issues and promoting well-being.
She pursued a degree in psychology within the Department of Psychology, fascinated by the complexities of human nature and the application of psychological principles.
The Department of Psychology faculty includes experts in various subfields, such as neuroscience, counseling psychology, and industrial-organizational psychology.
